LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ARTHUR ABEZGAUZ' MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS MET HIS BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING THAT HE LACKED ACTUAL NOTICE OF THE LAWSUIT, AND IT WAS NOT CAUSED BY AVOIDANCE OF SERVICE OF INEXCUSABLE NEGLECT. (CCP 437.5) PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTIONS TO DEFENDANT ABEZGAUZ'S DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION TO SET ASIDE ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS TIMELY AND IS ACCOMPANIED BY DEFENDANT'S AFFIDAVIT, AND PROPOSED ANSWER. (CCP 375.5(A).) DEFENDANT'S AFFIDAVIT STATES THAT HE HAD NO KNOWLEDGE OF THE LAWSUIT BEFORE RECEIVING THE ABSTRACT OF JUDGMENT, WHICH WAS DELIVERED TO THE ADDRESS 55 CASTLEMONT AVENUE, DALY CITY, CALIFORNIA. AS A CONDITION TO THE GRANT OF THIS MOTION, DEFENDANTS MUST PAY TO PLAINTIFFS, DELIVERED TO THE OFFICE OF PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL BY NOON ON FRIDAY, OCTOBER 4, 2013, REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,000. ANY ANSWER FILED WITHOUT COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER SHALL BE STRICKEN ON THE COURT'S MOTION OR ON EX PARTE APPLICATION. A VERIFIED DECLARATION SHOWING PAYMENT SHALL BE PRESENTED AND FILED AT THE TIME OF FILING THE ANSWER TO THE COMPLAINT. SEE CCP 473.5(C). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)

calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT ARTHUR ABEZGAUZ' MOTION FOR SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT is Granted. Defendant has met his burden of establishing that he lacked actual notice of the lawsuit, and it was not caused by avoidance of service of inexcusable neglect. (CCP 437.5) Plaintiffs' objections to Defendant Abezgauz's Declaration in Support of the Motion to Set aside are overruled. Defendant's motion is timely and is accompanied by Defendant's affidavit, and proposed answer. (CCP 375.5(a).) Defendant's affidavit states that he had no knowledge of the lawsuit before receiving the abstract of judgment, which was delivered to the address 55 Castlemont Avenue, Daly City, California. As a condition to the grant of this motion, defendants must pay to plaintiffs, delivered to the office of plaintiff's counsel by noon on Friday, October 4, 2013, reasonable attorney's fees in the amount of $1,000. Any answer filed without compliance with this order shall be stricken on the court's motion or on ex parte application. A verified declaration showing payment shall be presented and filed at the time of filing the answer to the complaint. See CCP 473.5(c). ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)
